title: Camp (canonical entity file)
description: One file per camp property: data/camps/<slug>.json. Registry + classification + history layer only — operational data (sessions, fees, dates, availability) stays in camp-finder / the council's own site, joined by camp id. A camp is classified on three orthogonal facets: camp_type (what kind of place, single), program_types (what it offers, set), and operator (+council; who runs it). Camp identity is independent of council ownership (camps change hands): council/operator are versioned attributes; sales/transfers are 'transferred' events, closures 'discontinued' events in data/camps/_events.json. Slugs keep camp-finder's state-prefixed convention (e.g. 'or-camp-meriwether').

camp_type.description: Primary structural classification (single; open vocab). Canonical set: reservation (a multi-camp property), resident_camp, high_adventure_base, short_term_camp (weekend/primitive troop use), day_camp, program_center, other. Multi-use is expressed via program_types, not here.

operator.description: Ownership/operation tier. 'council' pairs with the council ref below; 'national' = a National Council property (e.g. Philmont, Florida Sea Base, Northern Tier, Summit Bechtel) with council null. Disambiguates a null council (national base vs genuinely unknown).

council.description: Owning/operating council during this window; null = unknown (common for historical camps).

parent.description: The reservation this camp belongs to, as a camp ref (a sub-camp of a scout reservation); null if standalone. Reservation children are populated in a later pass.

operating_status.description: camp-finder semantics: 'not_operating' = property exists but no program; 'closed' = property gone/sold.

geo_precision.description: Confidence of lat/lon: 'exact' = camp-specific source point inside its state box; 'approximate' = city or state-centroid backfill (tools/geocode_camps.py); null = could not be placed. Consumers should soft-plot or bucket 'approximate' points.

elevation_ft.description: Ground elevation at lat/lon in feet above sea level (Copernicus 90m DEM via open-meteo, tools/elevation.py), rounded to the nearest 10 ft. Inherits geo_precision: for an 'approximate' point this is the city/reservation-centroid elevation, not the camp's exact ground. null when lat/lon is null.

website.description: Authoritative council/camp page during this window.

program_types.description: Programs/audiences offered here (open vocab, multi-valued): scouts_bsa_resident, cub_resident, cub_day, webelos, high_adventure, short_term_camping, venturing, sea_scout, family_camping, training. A single property is often multi-use.

features.description: Open vocabulary; seeded from camp-finder (dining_hall, waterfront, pool, shooting_sports, climbing, horseback, atv, cope, older_scout_program, high_adventure_option, stem, scuba, mountain_biking).

summary.description: Evergreen, original-prose description (setting, size, and the kinds of programs offered). MUST NOT contain dates, fees, session schedules, or other transitory details; enforced by tools/validate_data.py.

merged_from.description: Retired camp ids that resolve to this camp: program/session variants of the same physical property collapsed in, or a prior id this camp was renamed from. Any unioned programs/features live here; consumers resolve old ids via v1/camps/aliases.json.

reservation.description: Set when this camp shares a location with other distinct camps (a reservation centroid): a grouping so consumers render one reservation pin. `name` is null when the camps do not carry a common reservation name. Not a plottable entity.
